units.name: Corsair CV450
useCase: Entry Gaming (no dGPU or low-power GPU)
units.desc: Bare minimum reliable PSU for budget office builds or entry systems with no discrete GPU or a very low-power one like a GTX 1650. Non-modular cabling makes cable management harder in smaller cases.
cons: No headroom for any serious GPU upgrade

units.name: Cooler Master MWE 550 Bronze V2
units.desc: The standard budget gaming PSU recommendation - enough headroom for an RTX 4060 or RX 6600-class GPU paired with a mid-range CPU, with a reasonable 5-year warranty for the price point.

units.name: Corsair RM650x
useCase: RTX 4070/4070 Super, RX 7800 XT class builds
units.desc: One of the most recommended mid-range PSUs in the enthusiast community - fully modular cabling, excellent 80+ Gold efficiency, and a 10-year warranty reflecting genuine build quality. Comfortable headroom for RTX 4070-tier builds.

units.name: Seasonic Focus GX-750
units.desc: Seasonic's build quality is industry-respected - this unit comfortably powers RTX 4080-class builds with headroom for high core-count CPUs running at full load alongside the GPU during rendering tasks.

units.name: Corsair RM1000x SHIFT
units.desc: Built with the native 12VHPWR (ATX12VHPWR) connector required by RTX 4090/5090-class GPUs, removing the need for adapter cables that have caused melting issues on lower-quality units. Side-mounted connectors improve cable routing in modern cases.
cons: Overkill unless running a flagship GPU

units.name: ASUS ROG Thor 1200W Platinum
units.desc: Designed specifically for RTX 5090-class power draw with significant headroom remaining - suitable for overclocked extreme builds or dual-GPU AI/ML workstation setups. Native 12VHPWR connector and an OLED wattage display on the unit itself.
cons: Unnecessary for anything below flagship-tier GPUs
